topic: My smoke detector only beeps when no one is home
title: My Smoke Detector Only Beeps When No One Is Home and the App Knows | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: My smoke detector only chirps when no one is home. The app logs every time.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a white circular smoke detector mounted on a textured beige ceiling, a single small red LED faintly lit in the center, dim ambient hallway light from below, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Three bedroom townhouse. Outside Chicago. Bought it last fall.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior shot of a two story brick townhouse in a row of identical units at dusk, a small front porch with a single light on, mature trees along the sidewalk, no people, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: New construction. Wired alarms. Connected to a phone app.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a smartphone screen on a wooden kitchen table showing a smart home alarm app interface, a list of connected smoke detectors with green status dots, dim warm overhead light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I noticed the chirps last winter. Always during work. Never on weekends.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a smartphone screen showing an alarm event log, multiple weekday entries highlighted with timestamps between nine AM and five PM, the weekend rows blank, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I work nine to five. Eight miles from home.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of an empty modern office cubicle in afternoon light, a laptop open on the desk with a spreadsheet visible, a coffee mug beside it, fluorescent ceiling light reflecting on the desktop, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Detector in the hallway. Detector in the master bedroom. Always those two.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a second floor hallway in a modern townhouse, two doorways visible on either side, a white circular smoke detector mounted on the ceiling at the far end of the hall, ambient daylight from a window, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I checked the batteries. Brand new. No smoke detected ever.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a hand holding an open smoke detector cover and a fresh nine volt battery beside it on a stepladder shelf, the white plastic housing dismounted from the ceiling, dim overhead hallway light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Set up a phone camera on the dresser before work last Monday.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a smartphone propped on a wooden dresser with the camera lens facing the doorway, the screen showing a recording timer counting upward, the bedroom dim with morning light from a window, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Reviewed the footage that night. Chirp at eleven seventeen AM.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing video playback paused at a timestamp of eleven seventeen, the master bedroom visible in the frozen frame with the bed made and the room empty, the laptop on a kitchen table at night, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The bedroom door was closed when I left. In the video it slowly opened.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing video playback of a master bedroom doorway, the dark wooden door visibly partially open with a sliver of hallway behind it, no figure visible, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Something walked past the camera. Tall. Out of focus. Did not look at it.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing a paused video frame, a tall blurred dark shape moving past the foreground of the master bedroom near the dresser, the bed and window visible behind, the figure out of focus and indistinct, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The chirps stopped last week. Now the app says someone is home when I am not.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a smartphone screen showing a smart home app dashboard, a status banner reading HOME with a green indicator visible, the kitchen counter beneath the phone empty and dim, vertical 9:16 framing
